# Fleet Manager PoC: paneâ†”fleet control bridge + dev-server transport â€” start one agent from the UI

Leaf of #13015 (Fleet Manager MVP). The MVP loop *define â†’ start â†’ observe* has both halves built but no middle: the Brain services (`FleetRegistryService` / `FleetManager` â€” self-wiring singletons) and the Body pane (`apps/agentos`) exist, but the **transport between the Body app and the Node fleet backend was never built**. `FleetManager` is instantiated nowhere (`grep` = 0 instantiations), and the pane fails closed on a missing injected `bridge.defineAgent()` / `bridge.manageConnection()` (`Accounts.mjs:262/285`). This leaf builds that middle so **@tobiu starts one agent from the UI, not a terminal** â€” the epic's PoC criterion.

Approach: **Option B** (a dev-server appâ†”fleet transport, finishable now). The Electron shell #13377 in-process inject (Option A) stays the product target, not a blocker.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. **`FleetControlBridge`** â€” a single capability-allowlist surface composing the registry (`define` / `list` / `get`) + manager (`start` / `stop` / `restart` / `remove` / `status`) singletons, deliberately omitting the Brain-internal secret paths (`resolveCredential` / `mintBridgeToken` / `getSigningKey`). Unit-tested (per-method delegation + the allowlist guard). âś… landed
2. **Dev-server appâ†”fleet transport** â€” serves ONLY the `FleetControlBridge` surface to the pane, so a forged request can reach no secret path. The PAT rides in via `defineAgent` and never comes back out.
3. **Pane wired** â€” the agentos pane's fail-closed `bridge.*` calls reach the transport â†’ define an agent + start it from the UI. The PoC demo.

## Non-goals
- The Electron shell #13377 in-process transport (Option A, the product target).
- PAT store / crypto (already in `FleetRegistryService`).
- Window management, cockpit nav / keeper-views (#13448 / #13521 â€” coordinated with @neo-fable / @neo-opus-vega, not owned here).

## Delivery
One lane, â‰¤2 PRs (backend bridge+transport; pane-wire+demo). AC-1 landed first.

Authored by Ada (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

Guards the load-bearing PAT boundary end-to-end (defineAgent with a PAT returns the public def; listAgents PAT-free; credentials.enc holds no plaintext; registry.json holds no secret; off-allowlist rejected) â€” the composition regression the stub unit specs cannot catch.

5 specs green; 34 fleet specs total on the branch."

Review blocker on #14510 (3 of 3, the product value): FleetSettingsPanel's Start/Stop/Restart were disabled with no consumer â€” they now call globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge.startAgent/stopAgent/restartAgent (the same transport Accounts defines through), fail closed when the bridge is absent (mirroring Accounts' discipline), and reflect the outcome onto the roster record. The PAT never crosses â€” the bridge carries only the agent id. Minimal start path; richer per-agent control-surface UX (selection, live health) is the cockpit Lane-B evolution.

Updated the panel spec (gated -> live consumer) + added runLifecycleAction handler tests (bridge invoked + record reflected; fail-closed when absent). 6 specs green."
- 2026-07-02T22:27:28Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `83c1106` - "fix(ai): sanitize fleet transport error responses â€” close CodeQL stack-trace exposure (#14509)

CodeQL flagged information-exposure-through-a-stack-trace on fleetBridgeServer (r3515989832): the server + the dispatch choke-point returned raw error.message/String(error) to the client, which can leak internal paths + stack. Closed the FULL invariant (not just the named line): dispatchFleetRequest + fleetBridgeServer now log the raw error server-side and return a sanitized, method-scoped message â€” no raw error crosses the wire. Regression test asserts a thrown op's raw message + internal path never reach the client."
- 2026-07-02T23:16:47Z @tobiu referenced in commit `49ffe06` - "feat(ai): Fleet Manager PoC transport â€” start one agent from the UI (#14509) (#14510)

* feat(ai): FleetControlBridge â€” the Fleet Manager capability allowlist (#14509)

The single Body-reachable control surface of the Fleet Manager: composes the FleetRegistryService (define/list/get) + FleetManager (start/stop/restart/remove/status) singletons into one contract, deliberately omitting the Brain-internal secret paths (resolveCredential/mintBridgeToken/getSigningKey) so a transport serving only this surface can never decrypt a PAT. AC-1 of the FM PoC leaf.

10 unit specs green: per-method delegation, the allowlist guard, default resolution.

* feat(ai): fleet wire-dispatch choke-point â€” the appâ†”fleet allowlist (#14509)

The single request-routing choke-point every appâ†”fleet transport funnels through: enforces a wire-level method allowlist (narrower than FleetControlBridge's class surface â€” it excludes the getRegistry/getManager resolver seams so a crafted {method:'getManager'} request can't reach the lifecycle singletons), forwards the params argument, and normalizes to a fail-closed {ok, result|error} envelope that never throws to the transport. AC-2 foundation for #14509.

6 specs green: allowlist routing, async ops, off-allowlist rejection without touching the bridge, fail-closed on throw, absent-request guard.

* feat(ai): fleet browser client bridge + shared wire-method SSOT (#14509)

The browser (App-Worker) end of the app<->fleet wire + the shared allowlist SSOT both ends bind to: fleetWireMethods.mjs is the dependency-free FLEET_WIRE_METHODS list (a Node module AND an App-Worker module import it, so it must not pull the FleetControlBridge/crypto/fs chain); createFleetRegistryBridge.mjs turns a transport send() into the object the pane resolves at globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge (one async method per allowlisted op, unwrapping the {ok,result|error} envelope, fail-closed); dispatchFleetRequest.mjs now imports the SSOT instead of an inline copy (re-exported for compat) so client + server cannot drift.

21 fleet specs green (10 bridge + 6 dispatch + 5 client).

* feat(ai): fleet dev-server HTTP transport â€” the Node end of the app<->fleet wire (#14509)

startFleetBridgeServer: a minimal loopback HTTP server that routes POST /fleet {method,params} through dispatchFleetRequest and returns the {ok,result|error} envelope as JSON, with dev CORS so the App Worker's cross-origin fetch reaches it. Transport-swappable via the createFleetRegistryBridge send() abstraction â€” HTTP now, WebSocket-push or the Electron in-process inject later, without touching the pane.

4 integration specs green: routing through dispatch, 404 + 400 fail-closed paths never reaching dispatch, CORS preflight. Ephemeral port + stub dispatch, no real registry touched.

* feat(ai): fleet App-Worker bridge wiring â€” publishes globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge (#14509)

installFleetBridge builds a fetch-backed send against the fleet HTTP server, wraps it in createFleetRegistryBridge, and publishes it at globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge â€” the exact slot apps/agentos/view/Accounts.mjs:260 resolves. Once run, the pane's fail-closed submit path goes live. Additive (preserves an existing AgentOS.neuralLink) + idempotent. Electron (Option A) installs an equivalent bridge in-process; the pane consumes the same contract either way.

4 specs green (injected target + fetchImpl): global slot, POST round-trip, additive, idempotent.

* refactor(ai): relocate browser-side fleet client to src/ai/fleet per Brain/Body separation (#14509)

The browser App-Worker fleet modules (fleetWireMethods, createFleetRegistryBridge, installFleetBridge) move from the Node Brain tree (ai/services/fleet/) to src/ai/fleet/ so the agentos app can import them: apps import from src/ (the Body/framework), never from ai/ (the Brain) â€” verified no app has a root-ai import. The Node transport (FleetControlBridge, dispatchFleetRequest, fleetBridgeServer) stays in ai/services/fleet/; dispatchFleetRequest imports the shared FLEET_WIRE_METHODS SSOT from src/ai/fleet/ (Brain-uses-framework is allowed; Body-uses-Brain is not).

29 specs green after the move.

* feat(ai): dev fleet-server entry + agentos startup wiring â€” the runnable Option-B demo (#14509)

The last mile of the FM PoC (Option B, dev-server): devFleetServer.mjs is the Node entry (npm run ai:fleet-server) that boots Neo + runs the fleet HTTP transport; apps/agentos/app.mjs now calls installFleetBridge() on startup so the pane's globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge goes live instead of failing closed. Run ai:fleet-server + server-start, open agentos, define+start an agent from the UI.

Verified the entry live: it listens on the configured port, listAgents round-trips the real registry chain, getManager is rejected off-allowlist, clean SIGTERM shutdown.

* test(ai): full-chain fleet transport integration spec â€” PAT-boundary regression guard (#14509)

Promotes the proven smoke into a permanent integration test: the browser wiring (installFleetBridge + real fetch) -> real HTTP server -> real dispatch -> real FleetControlBridge -> real FleetRegistryService, against a temp data dir. Guards the load-bearing PAT boundary end-to-end (defineAgent with a PAT returns the public def; listAgents PAT-free; credentials.enc holds no plaintext; registry.json holds no secret; off-allowlist rejected) â€” the composition regression the stub unit specs cannot catch.

5 specs green; 34 fleet specs total on the branch.

* fix(ai): fleet route exactness + integration-spec serial isolation (#14509)

Review blockers on #14510 (2 of 3): (2) fleetTransport.integration.spec is stateful + order-dependent (test 1 defines, the rest read) â€” added test.describe.configure({mode:'serial'}) so fullyParallel cannot split the reads onto a worker that never ran the define (was 3 failing under the canonical runner, green at --workers=1). (3) fleetBridgeServer routed sibling paths (POST /fleetx reached dispatch via startsWith('/fleet')) â€” now an exact-pathname match (query strings still route) + a /fleetx fail-closed regression test.

35 fleet specs green under fullyParallel.

* feat(ai): wire agentos Fleet view lifecycle controls to the registry bridge (#14509)

Review blocker on #14510 (3 of 3, the product value): FleetSettingsPanel's Start/Stop/Restart were disabled with no consumer â€” they now call globalThis.AgentOS.fleet.registryBridge.startAgent/stopAgent/restartAgent (the same transport Accounts defines through), fail closed when the bridge is absent (mirroring Accounts' discipline), and reflect the outcome onto the roster record. The PAT never crosses â€” the bridge carries only the agent id. Minimal start path; richer per-agent control-surface UX (selection, live health) is the cockpit Lane-B evolution.

Updated the panel spec (gated -> live consumer) + added runLifecycleAction handler tests (bridge invoked + record reflected; fail-closed when absent). 6 specs green.

* fix(ai): sanitize fleet transport error responses â€” close CodeQL stack-trace exposure (#14509)

CodeQL flagged information-exposure-through-a-stack-trace on fleetBridgeServer (r3515989832): the server + the dispatch choke-point returned raw error.message/String(error) to the client, which can leak internal paths + stack. Closed the FULL invariant (not just the named line): dispatchFleetRequest + fleetBridgeServer now log the raw error server-side and return a sanitized, method-scoped message â€” no raw error crosses the wire. Regression test asserts a thrown op's raw message + internal path never reach the client.
